backend/main.mo: Motoko actor implementing the incident management backend with functions to create incidents, add updates, and change status, storing data in an in-memory map with severity and status variant types.
feature_evidence.json: Feature evidence report linking the dashboard cache-invalidation fix (React Query refetch after mutations) to the relevant source files and test requirement REQ-6.
frontend-file-summaries.txt: JSON document containing one-sentence summaries of every frontend source file, serving as a pre-built reference for the project's frontend structure.
frontend/index.css: Tailwind base stylesheet defining OKLCH-based CSS custom properties for light and dark themes covering background, card, border, and semantic color tokens.
frontend/index.html: Minimal HTML entry point titled 'District Status Page' that mounts the React app into a root div and loads the main TSX module.
frontend/src/App.tsx: Sets up TanStack Router with a root route wrapping the Layout component, an index route for the Dashboard, and an /admin route for the AdminPanel.
frontend/src/components/AddUpdateForm.ts: React Hook Form-based form for appending a timestamped update message to a selected incident, with an incident selector dropdown showing current status context.
frontend/src/components/ChangeStatusForm: Form for changing an incident's status via an incident selector and a status dropdown, displaying the current status for context before submission.
frontend/src/components/CreateIncidentFo: A React form component for creating new incidents, using react-hook-form for validation and checking for duplicate IDs before submitting via the createIncident mutation.
frontend/src/components/IncidentCard.tsx: Displays a single incident as a collapsible card showing severity, status, affected service, timestamps, description, and a sorted list of updates.
frontend/src/components/Layout.tsx: Provides the app shell with a sticky header (navigation links, logout button), main content area, and footer, using Internet Identity auth state to conditionally show the logout button.
frontend/src/components/SeverityBadge.ts: Renders a styled badge for incident severity levels (critical, major, minor, informational) with color-coded classes and an animated dot indicator.
frontend/src/components/StatusBadge.tsx: Renders a styled badge for incident status values (investigating, identified, monitoring, resolved) with distinct color schemes per status.
frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts: A hook that creates and caches an ICP backend actor (anonymous or authenticated) via React Query, and invalidates dependent queries whenever the actor changes.
frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.t: Provides an Internet Identity authentication context and hook, managing AuthClient creation, login/logout flows, identity persistence, and exposing status flags to consumers.
frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts: Defines React Query hooks for fetching and mutating incident data (get all, get one, create, add update, change status) using a backend actor, with cache invalidation on mutations.
frontend/src/index.css: Global CSS file that imports Inter and JetBrains Mono fonts, sets up Tailwind layers, and defines CSS custom properties for light/dark theme colors including status and severity color tokens.
frontend/src/main.tsx: Application entry point that mounts the React app with QueryClientProvider and InternetIdentityProvider wrappers.
frontend/src/pages/AdminPanel.tsx: Admin panel page gated behind Internet Identity login, providing tabs for creating incidents, adding updates, and changing statuses, with a login gate shown to unauthenticated users.
frontend/src/pages/Dashboard.tsx: Public-facing status dashboard that fetches all incidents, displays an overall status banner, and renders active incidents sorted by severity and resolved incidents in separate sections with loading skeletons.
frontend/tailwind.config.js: Tailwind CSS configuration extending the theme with custom OKLCH-based color tokens for UI, severity, and status states, custom fonts, border radii, box shadows, and accordion animations.
project_state.json: Project state metadata describing the district IT status page app, its existing features, architectural notes, and a known issue where the dashboard shows stale or missing incident data after admin updates.
